food_name: Chwee Kueh (Steamed Water Rice Cake with Chai Poh)

local_names.romanized_local: Shui Guo (Mandarin romanization); Chui Kueh (alternate romanization)

food_subgroup: Steamed rice flour snack cakes with preserved radish topping

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Chwee Kueh as a prepared Singaporean hawker snack. Closest parent would be a steamed rice-flour cake concept, which is not individually represented in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Steamed rice flour cake with savoury topping (closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code for Chwee Kueh with chai poh topping)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0KDA used as closest parent for steamed rice flour cake. No exact code for Chwee Kueh with preserved radish topping exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Chwee Kueh as a specific prepared food item.

preparation_method: topped with stir-fried preserved radish in oil

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker serving of chwee kueh is 3 steamed rice flour cups topped with chai poh (preserved salted radish in oil), weighing approximately 120g total.

nutrient_source.title: Chwee Kueh — HPB Singapore Foodo / Health Promotion Board Singapore Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chwee Kueh (3 pieces, ~120g). Values represent the complete dish including chai poh topping as sold at Singapore hawker stalls. All per-100g values are derived from this primary source.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rice-Based Foods — analogous estimate based on steamed white rice flour products; reference: University of Sydney GI Database (white rice and steamed rice flour products)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Chwee Kueh exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 is estimated by analogy to steamed white rice flour products and plain white rice (GI ~64–72, University of Sydney GI Database). The steaming process fully gelatinises the rice starch, producing a high GI comparable to plain white rice. GL per 120g serving calculated as: (70 × 30g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 21.0. Available carbohydrate per serving = 25.0 g/100g × 1.2 (120g serving) − 0.5g fibre/100g × 1.2 ≈ 30g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from chai poh (preserved salted radish) topping
hypertension.guidance: A standard 3-piece serving (~120g) provides approximately 600mg sodium — 25% of the 2,400mg daily limit — primarily from the chai poh topping; individuals with hypertension should request less chai poh or limit to one serving per day to manage cumulative sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) from fully gelatinised white rice flour starch with minimal fibre or protein to moderate absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of ~70 and GL of ~21 per 3-piece serving, chwee kueh raises blood glucose comparably to one cup of white rice; people with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size to 2 pieces, pair with a protein or vegetable side dish, and monitor postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate sodium (600mg/serving) and low saturated fat (2.6g/serving); primary cardiovascular concern is habitual high sodium intake from daily consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Chwee kueh is low in saturated fat and cholesterol, making it a relatively heart-friendly hawker option, but the ~600mg sodium per serving from chai poh warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease who must restrict daily sodium to under 1,500–2,000mg.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chwee Kueh (水粿, Steamed Water Rice Cake with Chai Poh) is a classic Singaporean Teochew Chinese hawker breakfast snack consisting of small, soft, bowl-shaped steamed cakes made from a thin batter of white rice flour and water, topped with chai poh — stir-fried preserved salted radish (菜脯) in oil, sometimes with garlic and chilli. The rice cakes are steamed in small metal or ceramic moulds, giving them a smooth, silky, slightly chewy texture with a mild, neutral flavour. The chai poh topping provides a salty, savoury, umami-rich contrast. A standard hawker serving is 3 pieces (~120g total). Per 100g: ~158 kcal, 2.5g protein, 5.8g fat, 25g carbohydrate, 500mg sodium. Per 3-piece serving (~120g): ~190 kcal, 3g protein, 7g fat, 30g carbohydrate, ~600mg sodium. The dish is gluten-free and dairy-free but may not be halal if lard is used in the chai poh preparation — customers should confirm with the stall. The primary clinical concern is sodium: the chai poh topping contributes approximately 75–80% of total sodium. GI is estimated at ~70 (high), GL ~21 per serving, comparable to one cup of white rice. Despite its high GI, chwee kueh is a low-calorie hawker option. It is a defining item of Teochew hawker culture in Singapore, commonly found at Bedok, Tiong Bahru, and Ghim Moh wet markets. Source: HPB Singapore Foodo database.
common_user_queries: how many calories in 3 pieces chwee kueh

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved source for Singapore hawker dishes. The food is an exact match in the HPB database. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using steamed white rice flour products as the reference analogue, which is well-supported and documented.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for chwee kueh with chai poh is not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this prepared dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB Singapore source). vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible/zero per HPB Singapore source for this rice-flour and preserved radish dish). Halal status is uncertain: chai poh may be stir-fried in lard at traditional hawker stalls — is_halal is set to false pending stall-level confirmation. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for chwee kueh exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per serving calculated as (70 × 30g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 21.0 for a 120g serving.
